channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: India Achieves Key Progress on Indigenous Fast Breeder Reactor at Kalpakkam
item.description: India’s nuclear programme has received international recognition as the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) has commended a major milestone achieved at the Indira Gandhi Centre for Atomic Resear...

item.title: Trinamool Congress Meets Election Commission Chief in a 15-Minute Discussion
item.description: A recent 15-minute meeting between Trinamool Congress representatives and the Chief of the Election Commission has sparked curiosity and concern in political circles. The closed-door meeting reportedl...

item.title: Fatal Crash Claims Life of Woman South of Brooks, Alberta
item.description: A tragic incident occurred south of Brooks, Alberta, on April 8, 2026, when a woman lost her life in a car crash. Alberta RCMP responded to the scene, but the details surrounding the cause of the coll...

item.title: Addressing Kenya’s Sh143 billion county revenue gap through fair allocation and fiscal reforms
item.description: The editorial sheds light on the severe financial crisis facing Kenyan county governments due to a Sh143 billion shortfall in revenue shares. Many counties are struggling to pay staff salaries, run es...

item.title: JD Vance emerges as a key voice for peace in US-Iran relations
item.description: JD Vance, the Vice President of the United States, has long positioned himself as a moderate voice within the Republican Party, particularly on matters of military intervention. Unlike many of his pee...

item.title: JM Ibarra and Fyang Smith Reflect on Bashing and Cancel Culture as Part of Their Careers
item.description: In a recent interview, celebrities JM Ibarra and Fyang Smith opened up about their experiences with online bashing and the phenomenon of cancel culture. Both stars acknowledged that these challenges h...

item.title: Elections BC fines BC United Party $4,500 for running false campaign against BC Conservatives
item.description: Elections BC, the independent provincial elections agency, has issued a $4,500 fine to the BC United Party for engaging in fraudulent election activities during the pre-campaign period ahead of the 20...

item.title: Nigerian Fashion Scene Needs Originality, Not Copies of Global Trends
item.description: The Nigerian streetwear scene is facing a crucial moment of introspection. While influenced by global trends, there's a growing recognition that true originality and identity are needed to elevate the...

item.title: Police investigate Fannie Nkosi over unauthorised possession of closed case files and firearms
item.description: Police in Pretoria are probing how suspended Sergeant Fannie Nkosi came to possess six case dockets, including files from cash-in-transit robberies and car hijackings, which were meant to be archived ...

item.title: David Mark-Led ADC Faction and Turaki-Led PDP Hold Strategic Meeting in Abuja

item.title: Trump voices strong criticism of NATO policies in meeting with Dutch leader
item.description: Former U.S. President Donald Trump sharply criticized NATO following a closed-door discussion with Dutch Prime Minister Mark Rutte, according to reports from Agence France-Presse. The meeting, held ea...

item.title: BJP fields no Muslim candidates for Bengal assembly elections, citing winnability concerns
item.description: The Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) has not nominated any Muslim candidates for the upcoming West Bengal assembly elections, marking a departure from previous years when the party fielded minority repres...

item.title: Activists raise concerns over exclusion of non-Hindu trans identities in updated law
item.description: The new Transgender Persons (Protection of Rights) Amendment Act, 2026, has raised alarm among activists for narrowing the legal recognition of transgender individuals in India. While the previous 201...

item.title: How to Stay Safe from Malicious QR Codes
item.description: QR codes have become an everyday convenience, allowing quick payments, menu access, and timetable updates without typing anything. However, this ease of use also attracts scammers. QR codes store info...

item.title: Parks Canada lifts motor boat ban at Clear Lake with new protection measures
item.description: Parks Canada has officially lifted the two-year ban on motorized boats at Clear Lake, located in Riding Mountain National Park in western Manitoba. The restriction was first introduced to prevent the ...

item.title: ADC Rivers Confirms Readiness To Hold Congresses Despite National Legal Disputes
item.description: The African Democratic Congress (ADC) for Rivers State don announce say dem no go cancel their ward, local government, and state congresses wey dem don already plan, even though palava still dey happe...

item.title: PSG Beat Liverpool 2-0 in First Leg of Champions League Quarter-Final
item.description: Paris Saint-Germain (PSG) outplayed Liverpool 2-0 in the first leg of their Champions League quarter-final on Wednesday, putting them in a strong position for the return leg at Anfield next week. PSG'...

item.title: Demand management is not enough to combat water scarcity in the Colorado River Basin
item.description: Water conservation efforts in cities like Phoenix, Denver, and Las Vegas have successfully reduced water use, but experts warn that climate change is rapidly outpacing these measures. The Colorado Riv...

item.title: Patient critically injured in alleged hospital hammer attack in Sydney
item.description: In the early hours of Thursday, emergency services were called to Royal Prince Alfred Hospital in Camperdown, Sydney, following reports of a violent assault. A 63-year-old male patient was allegedly a...

item.title: Respected Western Cape Judge Taswell Papier Dies
item.description: The Western Cape judiciary is mourning the loss of Judge Taswell Papier, a highly respected figure in the South African legal system, who passed away on Tuesday at the age of 64. Judge Papier dedicate...
